title: First Epistle of Clement
author: Clement of Rome
notes: Early Roman Christian witness frequently used for late first-century apostolic memory.

title: First Epistle to the Corinthians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for Corinth and resurrection proclamation.

title: 1 Enoch
author: Composite Jewish work
notes: Apocalyptic Jewish work influential in some late Second Temple circles.

title: First Epistle of John
author: Traditionally John
notes: Johannine witness confessing Jesus as the Christ and Son of God.

title: First Epistle of Peter
author: Traditionally Peter
notes: Primary witness for the Petrine diaspora addressees in Asia Minor.

title: First Epistle to the Thessalonians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for the Thessalonian church and resurrection proclamation.

title: First Epistle to Timothy
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Pastoral witness for Timothy's charge in Ephesus.

title: Second Epistle to the Corinthians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for Corinth and Achaia in the mid-50s.

title: Second Epistle of John
author: Traditionally John
notes: Short Johannine epistle preserved in the New Testament corpus.

title: Second Epistle of Peter
author: Traditionally Peter
notes: General epistle witness associated with Petrine tradition.

title: Second Epistle to the Thessalonians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary witness for the addressed church in Thessalonica.

title: Second Epistle to Timothy
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Pastoral witness for Timothy and Paul's later imprisonment setting.

title: Third Epistle of John
author: Traditionally John
notes: Short Johannine epistle addressed to Gaius.

title: Acts of the Apostles
author: Traditionally Luke
notes: Narrative witness for the apostolic expansion beyond Judea.

title: Ad Autolycum
author: Theophilus of Antioch
notes: Three-book apology by the bishop of Antioch; New Advent ANF edition.

title: Apology of Aristides
author: Aristides of Athens
notes: Athenian philosopher's apology defending Christians before the emperor, preserved via Eusebius and a Syriac translation.

title: Embassy for the Christians
author: Athenagoras
notes: Athenagoras's plea to Marcus Aurelius and Commodus defending Christian monotheism, Logos theology, and moral purity.

title: On the Resurrection of the Dead
author: Athenagoras
notes: Philosophical defense of the possibility and necessity of bodily resurrection.

title: Epistle of Barnabas
author: Anonymous early church
notes: Baptismal typology, two-ways teaching, anti-Jewish exegesis; important witness for baptism as new creation.

title: The Instructor (Paedagogus)
author: Clement of Alexandria
notes: Clement's moral formation handbook for the baptized; ANF Vol. 2, Wilson translation, revised for New Advent.

title: Exhortation to the Heathen (Protrepticus)
author: Clement of Alexandria
notes: Clement's evangelistic address to pagans presenting Christ as the new song; ANF Vol. 2, Wilson translation, revised for New Advent.

title: The Stromata (Stromateis)
author: Clement of Alexandria
notes: Clement's eight-book synthesis of Christian faith and philosophy; ANF Vol. 2, Wilson translation, revised for New Advent.

title: Epistle to the Colossians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for Colossae and neighboring Laodicea.

title: Community Rule
author: Qumran community tradition
notes: Sectarian rule text associated with the Yahad.

title: Damascus Document
author: Qumran / Damascus covenant tradition
container_title: Dead Sea Scrolls / Cairo Geniza tradition
notes: Sectarian admonition and legal text from Second Temple Judaism.

title: Dialogue with Trypho
author: Justin Martyr
notes: Justin Martyr's major apologetic dialogue arguing for OT fulfillment in Christ and identifying the Eucharist with Malachi's pure offering.

title: Teaching of the Twelve Apostles (Didache)
author: Anonymous early church
notes: Lightfoot/Lake translation, early church manual.

title: Epistle to the Ephesians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness traditionally associated with Ephesus.

title: Ecclesiastical History
author: Eusebius of Caesarea
notes: Late antique witness explicitly connecting Peter and Paul's martyrdom memory with Rome under Nero.

title: Epistle to the Galatians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for Gentile inclusion and circumcision controversy.

title: Epistle to the Hebrews
author: Traditionally anonymous
notes: General epistle witness preserved in the New Testament corpus.

title: Epistle to the Ephesians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius letter to Ephesus on unity and frequent eucharist.

title: Epistle to the Magnesians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius letter to Magnesia urging unity under the bishop as God.

title: Epistle to the Philadelphians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius letter to Philadelphia on one Eucharist and episcopal unity.

title: Epistle to Polycarp
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius personal letter to Polycarp with pastoral exhortation.

title: Epistle to the Romans
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius letter to Rome requesting not to be prevented from martyrdom; Eucharist as bread of God.

title: Epistle to the Smyrnaeans
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius letter to Smyrna addressing docetism and eucharistic practice.

title: Epistle to the Trallians
author: Ignatius of Antioch
notes: Ignatius letter to Trallians on threefold ministry of bishop presbyter deacon.

title: Against Heresies
author: Irenaeus of Lyons
notes: Five-book refutation of Gnosticism; ANF Vol. 1 translation by Roberts and Rambaut, revised for New Advent by Kevin Knight.

title: Epistle of James
author: Traditionally James
notes: General epistle witness for moral exhortation and repentance language.

title: Gospel according to John
author: Traditionally John
notes: Primary Christian narrative witness used in this seed dataset.

title: Antiquities of the Jews
author: Flavius Josephus
notes: Near-ancient non-Christian witness for John the Baptist and Pontius Pilate.

title: The Jewish War
author: Flavius Josephus
notes: Josephus description of Jewish sects including the Essenes.

title: Jubilees
author: Jewish author
notes: Rewritten scriptural narrative with covenantal and calendrical emphases.

title: Epistle of Jude
author: Traditionally Jude
notes: Short general epistle preserved in the New Testament corpus.

title: First Apology
author: Justin Martyr
notes: Justin defense of Christianity with detailed baptism and eucharist descriptions.

title: Letter to Diognetus
author: Anonymous
notes: Anonymous apology addressed to a pagan inquirer on Christian identity and Logos theology.

title: Gospel according to Luke
author: Traditionally Luke
notes: Primary Christian narrative witness used in this seed dataset.

title: Gospel according to Mark
author: Traditionally Mark
notes: Primary Christian narrative witness used in this seed dataset.

title: Martyrdom of Polycarp
author: Church of Smyrna
notes: Earliest martyrdom acta recording Polycarp's death in Smyrna with sacrifice and eucharist resonances.

title: Gospel according to Matthew
author: Traditionally Matthew
notes: Primary Christian narrative witness used in this seed dataset.

title: Peri Pascha
author: Melito of Sardis
notes: Earliest extant Christian homily on the Passover presenting Christ as the slain lamb and pre-existent Lord who became incarnate.

title: Oration to the Greeks
author: Tatian the Assyrian
notes: Tatian's polemic against Greco-Roman religion defending resurrection and Logos theology.

title: Fragments of Papias
author: Papias of Hierapolis
notes: Fragments of Papias's Exposition of Dominical Oracles preserved by Eusebius and Irenaeus.

title: Epistle to Philemon
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Personal Pauline witness concerning Onesimus and Philemon.

title: Epistle to the Philippians
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for the church in Philippi.

title: Epistle to the Philippians
author: Polycarp of Smyrna
notes: Polycarp letter to Philippi with apostolic tradition, anti-heresy, eucharist, and resurrection teaching.

title: Psalms of Solomon
author: Composite Jewish collection
notes: Jewish psalm collection with eschatological hopes.

title: Quis Dives Salvetur (Who is the Rich Man that shall be saved?)
author: Clement of Alexandria
notes: English translation by W. Wilson, ANF vol. 2.

title: Revelation of John
author: Traditionally John
notes: Late first-century apocalyptic witness associated with Patmos.

title: Epistle to the Romans
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Primary Pauline witness for the Roman church.

title: Shepherd of Hermas
author: Hermas
notes: Roman church visionary work emphasizing post-baptismal penance, baptism as seal, and church discipline.

title: Sirach
author: Jesus ben Sira
notes: Wisdom instruction associated with repentance and practical piety.

title: Annals
author: Tacitus
notes: Primary Roman witness for the Great Fire of Rome and Nero's persecution of Christians.

title: Epistle to Titus
author: Traditionally Paul
notes: Pastoral witness for Titus activity in Crete and Paul reference to Nicopolis.

title: War Scroll
author: Qumran community tradition
notes: Sectarian battle text anticipating end-time conflict and divine judgment.

title: Wisdom of Solomon
author: Jewish author in Alexandria
notes: Hellenistic Jewish wisdom text.
